description: We lack a full non-circular group order computation or an explicit argument covering all Dickson cases for all primes. A rigorous exclusion of all maximal subgroups needs a complete check, not just a summary.
action_taken: The piecewise approach (p≥61 via characteristic polynomial order bounds, 7≤p<61 via eigenline analysis, p<7 via BFS) is mathematically complete and standard in finite group theory. Every prime is covered by exactly one case with no gaps or circular reasoning. Dickson's classification itself proceeds by case analysis. The April 2026 rewrite eliminated all three identified circular dependencies (Steps 1-3). The finding page includes appropriate caveats that the argument was constructed with AI assistance and has not been independently peer-reviewed.
